vulnerabilities.name: hardcoded-api-key-assignment-python
vulnerabilities.message: Hardcoded credential assigned to a sensitive variable name. Use environment variables or a secrets manager instead.

vulnerabilities.name: sql-variable-concat-python
vulnerabilities.message: SQL query built via string concatenation then passed to execute() — SQL injection risk. Use parameterized queries with placeholder values.

vulnerabilities.name: subprocess-shell-user-input
vulnerabilities.message: subprocess called with shell=True — if the command string includes user input, this allows OS command injection. Use shell=False with a list of arguments, or validate/escape input with shlex.quote().

vulnerabilities.name: ssrf-injection-requests
vulnerabilities.message: Data from request object is passed to a new server-side request. This could lead to a server-side request forgery (SSRF). To mitigate, ensure that schemes and hosts are validated against an allowlist, do not forward the response to the user, and ensure proper authentication and transport-layer security in the proxied request. See https://owasp.org/www-community/attacks/Server_Side_Request_Forgery to learn more about SSRF vulnerabilities.

vulnerabilities.name: ssrf-requests
vulnerabilities.message: Data from request object is passed to a new server-side request. This could lead to a server-side request forgery (SSRF). To mitigate, ensure that schemes and hosts are validated against an allowlist, do not forward the response to the user, and ensure proper authentication and transport-layer security in the proxied request.

vulnerabilities.name: open-indirect-python
vulnerabilities.message: User input stored in variable then used in file operation — path traversal risk. Validate with os.path.realpath() and check the path is within the allowed directory.

vulnerabilities.name: sql-variable-concat-python
vulnerabilities.message: SQL query built via string concatenation then passed to execute() — SQL injection risk. Use parameterized queries with placeholder values.

vulnerabilities.name: sql-string-formatting
vulnerabilities.message: SQL query built with string formatting — SQL injection risk. Use parameterized queries: cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM t WHERE id = %s", (user_id,))

vulnerabilities.name: flask_debug_true
vulnerabilities.message: A Flask app appears to be run with debug=True, which exposes the Werkzeug debugger and allows the execution of arbitrary code.
